items.summary: The FCC has adopted rules to auction 160MHz of upper C-band spectrum by July 2027. #pressrelease

items.title: FCC Overhauls Satellite Licensing, Adopts ‘Assembly Line’ Approach

items.title: FCC approves sweeping space reforms while clearing more C-band for 5G
items.summary: The FCC voted July 22 to push satellite operators out of more C-band spectrum for a 5G auction next year, while approving a licensing overhaul aimed at accelerating the commercial space economy. The post FCC approves sweeping space reforms while clearing more C-band for 5G appeared first on SpaceNews .

items.title: AT&T braces for the agentic AI wave
items.summary: CEO John Stankey believes AT&T's networks are well positioned to manage the coming wave of agentic AI traffic amid investments in fiber, the network edge and spectrum that solidifies the wireless upstream.

items.title: T-Mobile US dominates Opensignal July network awards
items.summary: T-Mobile US won the most categories in Opensignal's latest network experience report, which also examined the performance of AT&T and Verizon over a 90-day period during a window which included the FIFA World Cup. items.title: AT&T posts record Q2 fibre, FWA additions
items.summary: AT&T reported a 2.3% year-over-year revenue increase to $31.6 billion in Q2, driven by net adds across fibre, fixed wireless access (FWA) and post-paid phone subscribers. The post AT&T posts record Q2 fibre, FWA additions appeared first on Mobile World Live .

items.title: Why Are We Building 6G?
items.summary: As 6G development accelerates, its promises of faster speeds, lower latency, and immersive applications raise questions about whether carriers can justify another costly network upgrade when 5G's commercial promises remain largely unfulfilled across the industry.

items.title: Ukrainian operators launch 5G pilot in Kyiv
items.summary: Ukraine operators Kyivstar, Lifecell and Vodafone switched on their latest open 5G pilot network in capital city Kyiv, the fourth location in the country and their largest 5G testbed to date. The post Ukrainian operators launch 5G pilot in Kyiv appeared first on Mobile World Live .

items.title: Miami broadband speed is slowest among US cities – Ookla
items.summary: At 113.51 Mbit/s, Miami claims the slowest median fixed download speed of any US city, according to Ookla's latest connectivity report. Miami's fastest ISP is AT&T Fiber, which again ranked as the fastest fixed network in the US.

items.summary: SpaceX's towering valuation rests less on Starlink's current connectivity economics than on ambitious platform expectations, raising doubts that satellite broadband and mobile services alone can justify trillion-dollar valuations indefinitely despite intensifying competition for investors.

items.title: Wi-Fi and Cellular Industries Duke It Out Over 6-GHz Band
items.summary: Global demand for high-capacity, low-latency wireless connectivity continues to grow, and the 6-gigahertz band of radio spectrum has emerged as a particularly vital chunk of wireless real estate. Both the cellular and Wi-Fi industries consider this contiguous block, which covers frequencies from 5,925 to 7,125...

items.title: AI Is Designing Radio Chips That Humans Couldn’t Even Imagine
items.summary: Summary RFIC design is a complex “ dark art ” that limits progress in wireless technologies like 5G, autonomous vehicles, and satellite communications. Princeton researchers use reinforcement learning and inverse design to rapidly create RFICs from scratch. Diffusion models rapidly generate novel or...

items.title: Home Broadband Is 5G’s Surprise Killer App
items.summary: 5G telecommunications, according to industry hype when 5G first launched in 2019 , was going to be all about buzzy applications like mobile augmented reality and autonomous vehicles . But the surprise plot twist came when replacing home cable internet turned into 5G’s most widely adopted new application. Fixed...

items.title: The Hidden 6G Bottleneck: RF Hardware Design Is Becoming a Strategic Race
items.summary: As 5G-Advanced matures and 6G research moves closer to implementation, the wireless industry faces a deeper challenge than spectrum, standards or AI-native network architecture. Future wireless systems will depend on whether the industry can design, validate and manufacture increasingly complex RF modules fast enough.

items.title: 6G in Dalian: What the Latest 3GPP Meetings Reveal About the Future Radio and Network
items.summary: The 6G physical layer is starting to converge. The protocol stack is being simplified in meaningful places. But the most consequential architecture decisions are now moving toward the June plenary in Singapore.

items.title: one6G Supports IEEE Communications Standards Magazine Special Issue on empowering robotics with 6G
items.summary: one6G is pleased to highlight the IEEE Communications Standards Magazine special issue, “Empowering Robotics With 6G: Connectivity, Intelligence, and Beyond.” Guest-edited by Mona Ghassemian, Kaspar Althoefer, Aryan Kaushik, Mahdi Tavakoli, Douwe Dresscher, and Xueli An, this publication […] The post one6G Supports...

items.title: Dell’Oro: Global RAN market finds stability as 5G, AI-RAN and early 6G investments take hold
items.summary: After a sharp multi-year downturn, the global RAN market is showing signs of recovery, with Dell’Oro Group forecasting modest growth through 2030 driven by 5G, AI-RAN and early 6G.

items.title: FCC sets up next C-band auction for 2027
items.summary: "America’s leadership in wireless is back," says Brendan Carr, FCC Chairman, as he announces 160 MHz of upper C‑Band to go to auction in 2027.

items.title: EchoStar off hook for operating own mobile network
items.summary: EchoStar is officially off the hook for building and running its own mobile network as Dish Wireless heads back to bankruptcy court this week.

items.title: Ericsson, LG Uplus target network-based voice AI for smarter calling
items.summary: Ericsson and LG Uplus are moving voice AI deeper into telecom networks, but have their work cut out for them to overcome privacy, latency and consent hurdles.

items.title: Fierce Network opens submissions for expanded 2026 Innovation Awards program
items.summary: Fierce Network has opened submissions for its 2026 Innovation Awards, introducing 14 new categories and bringing the awards ceremony to Broadband Nation Expo for the first time as it expands its focus on AI, network automation, cloud infrastructure and broadband innovation.

items.title: AT&T takes multi-prong approach to get out of California landline phone business
items.summary: One consumer advocacy group says AT&T has not provided any data or proof that its alternative telephone technology works in California’s most remote areas.

items.title: Fiber Demand, BEAD and AI Are Reshaping Broadband Deployment Planning
items.summary: AI growth and BEAD deployments are transforming fiber demand. Learn why proactive planning and early supplier partnerships are critical for success.

items.title: 'AI tailwind' keeps Arista at No. 1 spot in data center switching — now Nvidia looms
items.summary: Arista leveraged the AI buildout to take the No. 1 spot from Cisco in data center switching. Now it's facing off against the Nvidia AI colossus.

items.title: e& UAE and Core42 team to launch sovereign AI infrastructure
items.summary: The telco is teaming up with AI infrastructure provider Core42 to offer sovereign cloud services to enterprises in the UAE.

items.title: Cirion turns to agentic AI to optimize its Latin America network
items.summary: Cirion put Grokstream's Grok AIOps into production across 105,000 km of Latin American fiber, cutting alarm noise up to 92% and flagging faults early.
